title: Freezing Cancer Cell Lines
author: Emily Souster
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgtyjwpw

steps.title: Prepare 1 mL freezing media per vial as follows
steps.instruction: Prepare 1 mL freezing media per vial as follows: complete culture media + 10% DMSO.

steps.title: Detach and collect cells from a...
steps.instruction: Detach and collect cells from a flask, by following Steps 1-6 of the protocol: Passaging adherent cancer cell lines .

steps.title: Aspirate the supernatant, taking care to...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the supernatant, taking care to avoid disturbing the cell pellet. Resuspend the pellet in an appropriate volume of freezing media - depending on the number of vials being frozen. Mix well to ensure a single cell suspension. For example, if 5 vials are being frozen from a T150, resuspend the cell pellet in 5 mL of freezing media.

steps.title: Transfer 1 mL aliquots of the...
steps.instruction: Transfer 1 mL aliquots of the cell suspension to pre-labelled 1.8ml cryovials.

steps.title: Place vials in a 'CoolCell' or...
steps.instruction: Place vials in a 'CoolCell' or appropriate freezing container and store at -80 °C overnight. Appropriate freezing containers will ensure that the liquid freezes at a controlled rate of around -1 °C per minute at -80 °C .

steps.title: Transfer the vials to liquid nitrogen for long-term storage
steps.instruction: Transfer the vials to liquid nitrogen for long-term storage.
